title: Contributing to rminka
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("devMinka/rminka", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

text: Project-related functions illustrated using project Biomarató Tarragona 2025.

title: Introduction to rminka
text: Initially, project name known, search performed retrieve corresponding project ID. done mnk_proj_byname(). use query “biomarato 2025”.
code: prj_names <- mnk_proj_byname("biomarato 2025")  prj_names  #> # A tibble: 5 × 8 #>      id title      place_id slug  created_at updated_at project_type description #>   <int> <chr>         <int> <chr> <chr>      <chr>      <chr>        <chr>       #> 1   417 BioMARató…      244 biom… 2025-03-2… 2026-04-1… collection   "La BioMAR… #> 2   418 BioMARató…      245 biom… 2025-03-2… 2026-04-1… collection   "La BioMAR… #> 3   419 BioMARató…      249 biom… 2025-03-2… 2025-10-3… collection   "La BioMAR… #> 4   420 BioMARató…      248 biom… 2025-03-2… 2025-08-2… collection   "La BioMAR… #> 5   424 BioMARato…      701 biom… 2025-04-0… 2025-12-1… collection   "Descobre …  # For this example   prj_names[,c(1:2)] #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>      id title                      #>   <int> <chr>                      #> 1   417 BioMARató 2025 (Catalunya) #> 2   418 BioMARató 2025 (Girona)    #> 3   419 BioMARató 2025 (Tarragona) #> 4   420 BioMARató 2025 (Barcelona) #> 5   424 BioMARatona 2025

title: Introduction to rminka
text: Users explicitly subscribed project can retrieved mnk_proj_user() using project ID Note: simplest way retrieve participants project — including subscribed — download project’s observations mnk_proj_obs() extract unique values user_login. observation includes observer’s login, set distinct logins corresponds project’s participants. project lasts one year, interested participants single month, single function call sufficient. example, project 419 ran 2025, observations can obtained mnk_proj_obs(419, year = 2025):
code: prj_user <- mnk_proj_user(419)  prj_user #> # A tibble: 24 × 16 #>       id login          name              created_at          observations_count #>    <int> <chr>          <chr>             <dttm>                           <int> #>  1     4 xasalva        "xavi salvador c… 2021-04-16 10:44:11              81451 #>  2     6 ramonservitje  ""                2022-04-16 15:47:14               1259 #>  3    11 jaume-piera    "Jaume Piera"     2022-04-18 15:45:37              11059 #>  4    12 sonialinan      NA               2022-04-19 12:53:18                410 #>  5    13 adrisoacha     "Karen Soacha"    2022-04-21 09:40:57                578 #>  6    52 joselu_00      "José Luís Guijo… 2022-05-10 13:38:20                404 #>  7   159 jaumesaltiveri "Jaume Saltiveri" 2022-07-17 13:30:45                  2 #>  8   166 anomalia       "anomalia"        2022-07-19 07:56:08                 23 #>  9   197 peixderoca24   "Guillem Mayor S… 2022-08-08 12:58:18               4454 #> 10   219 ealcaniz       "Edu Alcaniz"     2022-08-27 15:52:52              27553 #> # ℹ 14 more rows #> # ℹ 11 more variables: identifications_count <int>, species_count <int>, #> #   activity_count <int>, journal_posts_count <int>, orcid <chr>, #> #   icon_url <chr>, site_id <int>, roles <list>, spam <lgl>, suspended <lgl>, #> #   universal_search_rank <int> # 1. Download all observations for the project in may 2025  obs_2025 <- mnk_proj_obs(419,year=2025, mont=5, quiet = TRUE)  # 2. Extract the unique participants  participants_may_2025 <- obs_2025 %>%   distinct(user_login) %>%   arrange(user_login)  participants_may_2025 #> # A tibble: 30 × 1 #>    user_login           #>    <chr>                #>  1 aci                  #>  2 albertvim            #>  3 allbluediving        #>  4 bertinhaco           #>  5 biosub               #>  6 elibonfill           #>  7 elisenda_casabona    #>  8 evararo              #>  9 francesca            #> 10 hectorserranocereijo #> # ℹ 20 more rows

title: Introduction to rminka
text: Returns observations project within selected year, optionally within specific month. Biomarató Tarragona 2025 (project ID 419), observations entire year obtained mnk_proj_obs(419, year = 2025), observations single month — example, May — obtained mnk_proj_obs(419, year = 2025, month = 5).

title: Introduction to rminka
text: Returns sf geometry place given place ID. place ID known, geometry can retrieved mnk_place_sf(). Piscinas del Forum, geometry obtained mnk_place_sf(253). example geometry visualized leaflet package using function’s default projection, WGS84 (EPSG:4326), standard used Google Maps. Note returned sf object already includes place name attribute.
code: # 1. Downloading the geometry   place <- mnk_place_sf(253)  # 2. Drawing the map   forum_sf <-leaflet(place) %>%                 addProviderTiles("OpenStreetMap", group = "OSM") %>%                 addProviderTiles("Esri.WorldImagery", group = "Satélite") %>%                 addPolygons(                   color = "#2c4fb8",                   weight = 2,                   opacity = 1,                   fillOpacity = 0.4,                   label = ~name, # information added from previous function                   highlightOptions = highlightOptions(weight = 3, bringToFront = TRUE)                 ) %>%                 addLayersControl(baseGroups = c("Satélite", "OSM"))   forum_sf

title: Introduction to rminka
text: core function package. highly versatile allows searches project, user, place, date, week number, taxon, geographic area. Two important notes apply mnk_obs() calls: query executed, message reports total number observations found. number exceeds 10,000, first 10,000 downloaded default. retrieve records, set limit_download = FALSE. retrieve validated records, use quality = "research".Previous examples already covered searches project, user place, focus two cases yet shown: ) search taxon b) search bounding box. Taxon search. Observations genus Raja species Raja brachyura 2025 obtained taxon_name = "Raja" taxon_name = "Raja brachyura" respectively. Images observations can visualized printed using magick package observation’s url_picture follow.  Bounding-box search. Observations within rectangular area retrieved supplying coordinates southwest northeast corners. example, search around Platja de Sant Sebastià Barcelona, define bounding box pass mnk_obs().
code:
#In this example don´t show messages in console (quiet= TRUE) # Get observations for genus Raja in 2025 by user Xavier Salvador ( user_id=4) obs <- mnk_obs(taxon_name = "Raja", year = 2025, user_id = 4, quiet = TRUE, quality = "research") obs # taxon_threatened , taxon_introduced , taxon_native , #> # user_id , user_login # Get observations for species Raja brachyura in 2023 by user Xasalva (user_id= 4) obs_brachyura <- mnk_obs(taxon_name = "Raja brachyura", year = 2023, user_id = 4, quiet = TRUE, quality = "research") obs_brachyura[, c(1,2, 10, 11, 16, 27)] # Get observations for specie Raja undulata in 2022 by user Xasalva (user_id= 4) obs_undulata <- mnk_obs(taxon_name = "Raja undulata", year = 2022, user_id = 4, quiet = TRUE, quality = "research") # select 3rd observation and get picture URL (url_picture) url_pic_undulata <- as.character(obs_undulata$url_picture[3]) # read image directly from URL img_undulata <- image_read(url_pic_undulata ) # add copyright caption at top img_undulata <- image_annotate( img_undulata, "© Xavier Salvador", size = 10, gravity = "northwest", color = "white", ) # title: Get Direct Access to Minka Data
text: rminka toolkit interacting Minka API, providing consistent set functions help query retrieve biodiversity data. package’s functions grouped type data return: ) User Queries: Functions find users contributed observations. mnk_user_byname(): Finds user’s ID ( user_id) approximate login name. mnk_user_info(): Retrieves detailed user information using known ID (user_id) mnk_user_proj(): Find projects user explicitly subscribed based user_id. mnk_user_obs(): Retrieves observations contributed user given year ( year specific month) user_id. b) Project Queries: set complementary functions find projects associated observations. mnk_proj_byname(): Finds project’s ID (project_id) using approximate project name. mnk_proj_info(): Retrieves detailed project information using known ID (project_id). mnk_proj_user(): Find users, associated metadata, explicitly subscribed given project project_id. mnk_proj_obs(): Fetches observations specific year within project. c) Place Queries: Functions find places retrieve spatial data. mnk_place_byname(): Finds place´s ID (place_id) location using approximate place name. mnk_place_sf(): Returns sf geometry place given place_id, ready plotting packages like ggplot2 leaflet. mnk_place_obs(): Retrieves observations place given year (year specific month) place_id . d) Observation Queries: variety functions fetch observation data based different parameters. mnk_obs_id(): Retrieves single observation’s complete data using unique ID (id). mnk_obs(): Fetches observations based various parameters full year, specific month, single day. mnk_obs_byday(): Retrieves observations within date range year. e) Auxiliary functions: set functions different utilities complement Minka’s observational data help processing used R packages (vegan, dismo, labdsv others). export_mnk_qgis(): Converts one sf layers different geometry types (points polygons) GeoPackage (.gpkg) format usualy used Qgis. default, uses CRS=4326 (WGS 84), function can transform different CRS. mnk_obs_sf(): function converts Minka observation tibble geometric sf layer. function allows select information contain (none default). also uses CRS=4326 (WGS 84) default, function can transform different CRS. get_wrm_tax(): Retrieves complete taxonomy additional data (e.g., marine terrestrial ) WoRMS given scientific name. shrt_name(): Returns CEP name (abbreviated scientific name) separation point, given scientific name. functions designed used together. queries span multiple years, can easily loop years interest, run appropriate function, combine resulting tibbles dplyr::bind_rows().

what: Export sf Minka Objects to a GeoPackage for QGIS — export_mnk_qgis
title: Export sf Minka Objects to a GeoPackage for QGIS — export_mnk_qgis
text: Writes one sf objects GeoPackage (.gpkg) one layer per object. function designed smooth QGIS workflow: ensures valid geometries, drops Z/M dimensions, removes list-columns GDAL write, transforms target CRS.

title: Export sf Minka Objects to a GeoPackage for QGIS — export_mnk_qgis
code: export_mnk_qgis(..., file = NULL, crs = 4326, overwrite = TRUE)

title: Export sf Minka Objects to a GeoPackage for QGIS — export_mnk_qgis
text: ... One objects class sf. object must named; name used layer name GeoPackage (e.g., export_mnk_qgis(points = pts, polygons = polys)). file Character. Path output GeoPackage. highly recommended provide full path. simple filename provided, saved current working directory. .gpkg extension added missing. crs CRS transform writing. Accepts anything valid sf::st_transform() (e.g., 4326 "EPSG:4326"). Default 4326 (WGS 84). overwrite Logical. TRUE file exists, file deleted writing. FALSE file exists, function appends layers (error layer name already exists). Default TRUE.

title: Export sf Minka Objects to a GeoPackage for QGIS — export_mnk_qgis
text: Invisibly returns normalized path written GeoPackage. Called side effect writing file.

title: Export sf Minka Objects to a GeoPackage for QGIS — export_mnk_qgis
text: function performs following steps layer: Transform crs sf::st_transform(). Drop Z M dimensions sf::st_zm(). Repair invalid geometries sf::st_make_valid(). Remove non-geometry list-columns, GDAL write GPKG. Write layer sf::st_write() using GPKG driver. overwrite = TRUE, existing file removed first. file removed (e.g., open QGIS), function stops informative error.

text: scientific_name character string scientific name search , example "Diplodus sargus" "Diplodus".

title: Get WoRMS Taxonomy — get_wrm_tax
text: one-row tibble taxonomic habitat flags. Returns NULL invisibly taxon found request fails. tibble contains following columns: valid_AphiaID WoRMS AphiaID, integer. valid_name accepted scientific name, character. rank taxonomic rank, character. kingdom kingdom, character. phylum phylum, character. class class, character. order order, character. family family, character. genus genus, character. isMarine marine flag, logical. isBrackish brackish flag, logical. isFreshwater freshwater flag, logical. isTerrestrial terrestrial flag, logical. isExtinct extinct flag, logical.

title: Download Minka Observations — mnk_obs
text: Downloads observation data Minka API. Handles pagination rate limits automatically subdividing large queries month day.

title: Download Minka Observations — mnk_obs
code: mnk_obs(   query = NULL,   taxon_name = NULL,   taxon_id = NULL,   user_id = NULL,   project_id = NULL,   place_id = NULL,   endemic = NULL,   introduced = NULL,   threatened = NULL,   quality = NULL,   geo = NULL,   annotation = NULL,   year = NULL,   month = NULL,   day = NULL,   bounds = NULL,   quiet = FALSE,   limit_download = TRUE )

title: Download Minka Observations — mnk_obs
text: query generic query string 'q' parameter. taxon_name character string taxon name (common scientific). taxon_id numeric ID taxon. user_id numeric ID specific user. project_id numeric ID specific project. place_id numeric ID specific place. endemic logical value. Filters endemic species. introduced logical value. Filters introduced species. threatened logical value. Filters threatened species. quality character string. Must 'casual' 'research'. geo logical value. TRUE, filters observations coordinates. annotation numeric vector length 2 (term_id, term_value_id). year numeric value year. month numeric value month (1-12). day numeric value day (1-31). bounds bounding box. Accepts sf object CRS EPSG:4326 (WGS84) numeric vector c(nelat, nelng, swlat, swlng). quiet logical value. TRUE, suppresses console messages. limit_download logical value. TRUE (default), caps download 10,000 records.
